title: Tantalizing Charred Japanese Cucumber Shoyu Ramen
description: A crisp, smoky ramen featuring charred Persian cucumber and zesty shoyu, served in lacquerware bowls for a Zen-inspired experience.
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium-high heat and char cucumber slices until lightly blackened, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer charred cucumber to a bowl and set aside.
In a saucepan, bring kombu stock to a gentle simmer, whisk in soy sauce, mirin, and sesame oil, and let it thicken slightly for 5 minutes.
Add ramen noodles to the broth and cook until al dente, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the charred cucumber slices and let the flavors meld for 1 minute.
Ladle the ramen into lacquerware bowls, ensuring noodles and broth are evenly distributed.
Garnish each bowl with pickled cucumber, scallion slivers, toasted sesame seeds, and a thin sheet of nori.
Serve hot and enjoy the crisp, smoky bite of the cucumber with savory shoyu ramen.
